articles.title: Kerala State Disaster Management Authority issues heat warning for election workers - The Hindu
articles.full_text: Amid the rising daytime temperatures, the Kerala State Disaster Management Authority (KSDMA) has issued a warning advising public workers, the general public, and officials involved in election campaigns to be cautious. Prolonged exposure to direct heat on the body can cause health problems such as sunstroke, heatstroke, and dehydration. Therefore, those involved in election activities should take adequate safety precautions, the agency said.
